# Groundcheck

> Claim grounding and citation attestation for AI agents. Groundcheck is verified enrichment: it grounds factual claims against live web sources (Wikipedia + world news) and returns a machine-verified verdict, a confidence score, and cited sources — over MCP or HTTP, so any agent can call it mid-task. Identity attestation (x401, ERC-8004) verifies who an agent is; Groundcheck verifies what it says.

## When to use
- Before asserting a fact you are not certain of (a date, number, name, statistic): call `verify_claim`.
- Before publishing AI-generated text: call `check_citations` to verify every claim in the draft.
- To signal that content was checked: call `attribution_badge`.
- Before acting on a claim, order, or document that names a security: call `resolve_instrument` to know exactly WHICH instrument it is about (ticker/ISIN/CUSIP/SEDOL/FIGI/name -> canonical FIGI records with provenance).

## Remote MCP (no install)
- Add the hosted engine as a remote MCP server by URL: https://groundcheck.seiche.info/mcp — speaks streamable-HTTP JSON-RPC (initialize, tools/list, tools/call). Works in Claude/ChatGPT/Cursor connectors and MCP gateways (Smithery, Glama). verify_claim is free; check_citations and resolve_instrument are x402-paid with the same free daily quota as the REST paths.

## Install
- Engine (Python, FastAPI): `make install && export GROQ_API_KEY=... && make engine` (runs on :8723)
- MCP server (TypeScript): `claude mcp add groundcheck -- npx -y groundcheck-mcp`
- The MCP server talks to the engine over HTTP (GROUNDCHECK_ENGINE_URL, default http://127.0.0.1:8723).
- Source & docs: https://github.com/beepboop2025/groundcheck

## Tools
- verify_claim(claim, maxSources) -> { verdict, confidence, rationale, sources }
- check_citations(text, maxClaims) -> per-claim verdict report
- attribution_badge() -> markdown badge
- resolve_instrument(query, idType?, maxResults) -> { matched, instruments[] (FIGI, name, type, sector, exchange), provenance } — Bloomberg open symbology (OpenFIGI v3). verify_claim also auto-resolves explicit $TICKER / ISIN / FIGI references in claims and reports them in `instruments`.

## Pricing (x402, hosted engine only)
- Running your own engine is free, always. Hosted deployments MAY enable x402 pay-per-call.
- POST /verify (single claim): always free, rate-limited.
- POST /check (batch document check): free daily quota per IP, then USD 0.02 per call in USDC.
- POST /resolve (attested instrument identity): same free daily quota, then USD 0.005 per call in USDC.
- Payment: x402 protocol, v1 and v2 both accepted (X-PAYMENT or PAYMENT-SIGNATURE header), "exact" scheme, USDC on Base (eip155:8453). No account or API key.
- Machine-readable manifest: GET /.well-known/x402 on any x402-enabled deployment.
- Flow: POST /check -> HTTP 402 with payment requirements -> sign EIP-3009 USDC authorization -> retry with payment header -> result + settlement receipt in X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE / PAYMENT-RESPONSE.
